general_hypothesis: Controlled interventions license progressively stronger attribution claims only when replay identity, observed divergence, propagation, paired repair, alternative sufficient causes, and collateral checks are independently evidenced.
useful_completion: A deterministic two-shape matrix distinguishes injected delta, first divergence, propagated surface, but-for repair, earliest-sufficient, natural-root, and repair-utility rungs without promoting construction labels.
fail_closed_rules: Replay-diverged attempts are invalid and excluded from attribution scoring but retained in denominators.
Prefix-only observations cannot support propagated consequence, but-for repair, earliest-sufficient cause, natural root, or repair utility.
A construction intervention may not be promoted to earliest-sufficient cause without exclusion of earlier causes and a sufficient matched repair.
No builder-authored synthetic intervention may be labeled a natural-failure root.
Dual-fault cases must preserve alternative sufficient causes rather than force one root.
Repair utility requires paired outcome recovery and no regression on the declared unaffected control.

claim_limits.supported: Exact deterministic behavior of the frozen policies on builder-authored equivalent forms, including local claim-rung and fail-closed semantics.
